title: ACDP Lineage-Head Receipt (0.3.0)
description: A registry-signed attestation that, as of a stated time, a stated context version was the current head of a stated lineage with a stated status. Specified by RFC-ACDP-0011 (acdp/0.3.0), extending the RFC-ACDP-0010 receipt layer to serve-time claims; additive minor, so this schema lives in the v0.1.0 namespace (VERSIONING.md). The receipt preimage is the JCS canonicalization of this object minus 'signature'; the registry signs the ASCII bytes of 'sha256:<hex(SHA-256(preimage))>' exactly as RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.8 / RFC-ACDP-0010 §5, with the RFC-ACDP-0010 receipt signing key.

receipt_version.description: Head-receipt envelope version and in-preimage domain separator (RFC-ACDP-0011 §4): a lineage-head receipt can never be mistaken for an RFC-ACDP-0010 context receipt.

registry_did.description: The attesting registry's DID. MUST be did:web:<authority> where <authority> equals the serving authority (RFC-ACDP-0011 §4, §7 step 3). did:web-only, as RFC-ACDP-0010 §4.

lineage_id.description: The attested lineage (RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.6). MUST equal the requested lineage / the accompanying body.lineage_id (RFC-ACDP-0011 §7 step 4).

head_ctx_id.description: The ctx_id of the head version. Its authority MUST equal the registry_did method-specific identifier (RFC-ACDP-0011 §4).

head_status.description: The head's registry-derived status at as_of (RFC-ACDP-0004 §4). Never 'superseded' — a superseded version is never the current head (RFC-ACDP-0004 §5.2) — and (0.3.0) never 'retracted' — a retracted version is never served as head (RFC-ACDP-0013 §8.3). In practice 'active' or 'expired'. Open pattern for future status values, as RFC-ACDP-0004 §4.1.

as_of.description: Registry response-time clock reading when the head claim was evaluated; canonical millisecond-precision RFC 3339 UTC (RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.3). Consumers reject a future as_of beyond a small skew allowance and apply their own max-age freshness policy (RFC-ACDP-0011 §6, §7 step 6).

signature.description: The registry's signature over the receipt hash (RFC-ACDP-0011 §5 — the RFC-ACDP-0010 §5 construction verbatim, same receipt signing key). signature.key_id MUST be a DID URL under registry_did; key lifecycle per RFC-ACDP-0010 §9.

$comment: Closed schema, as acdp-registry-receipt.schema.json (RFC-ACDP-0007 §3.3.1 openness map): every field is signed, so an unknown member would change the preimage. Extensions require a receipt_version bump.
